title: Wheres mah battery
wikitext:
[]: Where's mah battery?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:51 pm The characters keep mentioning that the Evas have internal batteries. Where exactly are they in the Evas? It can't be the torso, because Shamshel would have destroyed Eva-01's battery in EP 3.

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:53 pm unlike the cable i don't think the batteries have to be in an obvious spot. they could hide the battery anywhere really...don't even think about it...

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:54 pm I always got the impression the batteries were either somewhere in the Entry Plug mechanism, or in the base of the shoulder pylons...but that's just me...

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:56 pm ^ yeah i'd agree with that

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:58 pm I thought that the entry-plug had the battery in it. Either that or the entry-plug is the battery, and that the pilot sits inside it.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Sep 09, 2009 10:55 pm I can think of one place the battery could be stored. EDIT: In case you don't get it I am implying the place where the sun don't shine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Sep 09, 2009 10:58 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: I can think of one place the battery could be stored.

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Wed Sep 09, 2009 11:10 pm I always thought the battery was located somewhere below the entry plug or maybe below the socket for the cable.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Sep 09, 2009 11:12 pm Mongoosedog wrote: unlike the cable i don't think the batteries have to be in an obvious spot. they could hide the battery anywhere really...don't even think about it...

[]:             Quote Post by Rock-Slash » Wed Sep 09, 2009 11:22 pm In ep11 the external batteries were place it over the shoulder pylons so, is likely that the internal ones are inside the pylons.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Sep 10, 2009 9:50 am Spine idea: Shammy would have impaled the battery when it stabs Eva-01 in EP3. Pylon: Aren't the pylons already carrying the knives, boosters, and spike launchers?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Sep 10, 2009 11:23 am CyberXIII wrote: Spine idea: Shammy would have impaled the battery when it stabs Eva-01 in EP3. Pylon: Aren't the pylons already carrying the knives, boosters, and spike launchers?

[]: Re: Where's mah battery?             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 10, 2009 11:27 am CyberXIII wrote: Where exactly are (the batteries) in the Evas?

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Sep 10, 2009 8:25 pm Ornette wrote: CyberXIII wrote: Spine idea: Shammy would have impaled the battery when it stabs Eva-01 in EP3. Pylon: Aren't the pylons already carrying the knives, boosters, and spike launchers?

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Fri Sep 11, 2009 6:00 pm I could be wrong about this, but I thought there were two occasions where we see Unit-00 in one of NERV's testing facilites without the shoulder pylons attached. The first was early on (Unit-00 was still painted orange) in a flashback sequence of an activation experiment when Rei is hurt after her entry plug crashes on the floor and the commander burns his hands on the emergency levers while openning the plug. The other time was during the cross compatibility experiments (Unit-00 now painted blue) and they were trying to determine whether Shinji could be a vialble pilot for Unit-00. IIRC in both incidents Unit-00 goes beserk and, after severing the external power supply, they still have to wait about thirty seconds until internal battery power runs out before they can regain control of the situation. If this is correct, I guess it could mean that either the internal batteries are not located in the shoulder pylons, or that there are multiple locations hidden within the armor for backup batteries. Of course, I guess it might also mean that battery location is not the same on the prototype...

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Sat Sep 12, 2009 7:42 pm BC Baron wrote: I could be wrong about this, but I thought there were two occasions where we see Unit-00 in one of NERV's testing facilites without the shoulder pylons attached. The first was early on (Unit-00 was still painted orange) in a flashback sequence of an activation experiment when Rei is hurt after her entry plug crashes on the floor and the commander burns his hands on the emergency levers while openning the plug. The other time was during the cross compatibility experiments (Unit-00 now painted blue) and they were trying to determine whether Shinji could be a vialble pilot for Unit-00. IIRC in both incidents Unit-00 goes beserk and, after severing the external power supply, they still have to wait about thirty seconds until internal battery power runs out before they can regain control of the situation. If this is correct, I guess it could mean that either the internal batteries are not located in the shoulder pylons, or that there are multiple locations hidden within the armor for backup batteries. Of course, I guess it might also mean that battery location is not the same on the prototype...

[]:             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Sun Sep 13, 2009 3:23 pm Blast. I knew I should have charged the batteries before I left the house this morning.

[]:             Quote Post by Orphan Of Darkness » Sun Sep 13, 2009 7:42 pm I'm going to go ahead and say they are on the backs of the Eva's near or part of the socket for the power cables. They seem to have a lot of gear back there regarding their operations.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Sep 14, 2009 4:59 pm Evas have three abdominal pairs of organs that produce electricity: the Main organ, the Hunter's organ, and the Sachs organ. These organs comprise 60% of its body and appear as musculature to the untrained eye. These organs are made of electrocytes, lined up so that the current flows through them and produces an electrical charge in a manner similar to a battery, in which stacked plates produce an electrical charge. In the Eva, some 500,000 to 600,000 stacked electroplaques are capable of holding a charge of up to 50,000 teravolts and 30,000 terawatts of current. The Sachs organ is associated with entry plug operations. Inside the organ are many muscle-like cells, called electrocytes. Each cell can only produce 1,500 V, though working together the organ transmits a signal of about 10,000 V in amplitude at around 250 Hz. While not designed to generate electricity on their own, the otherwise unexplained activity of a Berserk Eva can be attributable to unanticipated spontaneous power generation in these cells, which are largely based on those found in the electric eel. </bs>

[]:             Quote Post by Apjak » Tue Sep 15, 2009 10:15 am Where's the alternator?

[]:             Quote Post by Mongoosedog » Wed Sep 09, 2009 9:53 pm unlike the cable i don't
